I just tried to reproduce this with macos instead of Linux and could not repro.
1. `flutter build macos --debug`
2. `build/macos/Build/Products/Debug/my_app.app/Contents/MacOS/my_app`
3. Run `dart devtools` and connect to the `my_app`

I think this is a duplicate of https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/6848, which was fixed by https://github.com/flutter/devtools/pull/7298.

146864.comment_body:
Thanks for the report. Reproduced the issue on the latest Flutter stable and master channels. Some additional info: - Issue doesn't happen with `TextField` - If I use `CupertinoApp` with `CupertinoThemeData(brightness: Brightness.dark)` to theme, issue will not happen as well but we will not achieve application theme by there is no `themeMode` on CupertinoApp. - Related issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/80860

146986.comment_body:
Reproducible using the code sample provided above. 

<details>
<summary>complete sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() async {
  runApp(const PageDemo());
}


class PageDemo extends StatefulWidget {
  const PageDemo({super.key});

  @override
  State<PageDemo> createState() => _PageDemoState();
}

class _PageDemoState extends State<PageDemo> {
  final PageController controller = PageController();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    if (controller.hasClients) {
      print(controller.page);
    }
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: PageView(
          controller: controller,
          children: [
            // Only throws if a builder or sub widget is used here.
            Builder(
              builder: (context) {
                final double currentPage =
                    controller.hasClients ? controller.page ?? -1 : -2;
                return Center(child: Text(currentPage.toString()));
              },
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),

So it looks like this is only an iOS issue.  
lowering the priority since it's iOS only
@kevmoo  yes, I'm using voiceover on mac
@kevmoo verified with Yegor this is working on windows with JAWS and NVDA.

I tried to fix this issue, but I couldn't get the existing tests to pass. Therefore, I am unassigning myself (I apologize for any confusion this may cause). I believe the solution should be here:

From:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7130981fe1ca12481f0bd5c4bdf6dbcee84afe43/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/segmented_button.dart#L632-L638

To:

```dart
return MergeSemantics(
        child: Semantics(
          selected: segmentSelected,
          inMutuallyExclusiveGroup: widget.multiSelectionEnabled ? null : true,
          child: buttonWithTooltip,
        ),
      );
```

It's replacing `checked` to `selected`, then the state can be announced as expected. It's `replacing` action but not `adding` because `adding` will cause the button to be read with both `checked` and `selected` states with TalkBack reader (tested this).

> Do you still remember which tests will fail?

@QuncCccccc Yes, they are here, but when I try to reproduce the failure again, I fortunately managed to resolve them. Perhaps I was a little confused earlier. I will try again. 😅

> FYI @gaaclarke , I thought you made some changes so that we would hold the callbacks until the GPU context was available again?
> 
> Needs investigation.

There is a mechanism for that.  From memory I think there are potential use cases that could opt into that that we didn't invest into, instead relying on sync switches.

@jonahwilliams Here's the PR:  https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/46840

It implements retry for `toByteData`, not `toImage`.

Here's the stacktrace for creating the image.  It's going to be a bit tricky to schedule it for when the GPU becomes available since the mechanism exists on `impeller::Context` which we don't have access to here.

TextFormField ignores it if the `initialValue` is changed because - as the name implies - it is only used to initialize the value for the very first time. So, when `TextFormField(initialValue: "loading...")` is changed to `TextFormField(initialValue: "10")` this is by design not reflected in the UI. If you want to change the value displayed in a TextFormField (or any TextField for that matter) you have to provide it with a TextEditingController and modify its value.

147279.comment_body:
Hi @XuanTung95, what device are you seeing this on? I tested on my pixel 7 but I can't see the fonts "jumping". See the recording below 


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/88313112/7c17d09d-f6b7-4ef4-aebf-c7f0dfcfe624

I can reproduce it now, I used the google_fonts package but forgot to call `WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized()` before the app ran which is why it didn't reproduce earlier. I didn't see this issue on macOS or iOS or web. 

Labeling for further investigation

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/88313112/6e5a3c0a-dd6b-4c27-8f3a-9a51be8b2feb


<details>
<summary>sample used </summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:google_fonts/google_fonts.dart';

void main() {
  WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();

  runApp(
    MaterialApp(
      theme: ThemeData(
        fontFamily: GoogleFonts.inter().fontFamily,
      ),
      home: const Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: TextField(obscureText: true),
        ),
      ),
    ),
  );
}
```
</details>

The issue with original code https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/147469#issue-2267076870 is that registered isolate  exits once it receives first message, so no further sends will work. It's confusing of course that the error talks about invalid object and only shows up if it's an instance of user-defined class being sent, it silently does nothing if primitive object is being sent.

> Is it expected for send to fail in case the port is closed/not available?

No, it's not. Send should not behave differently whether port is open or closed. The underlying root cause of the exception is gap in the dart vm api [Dart_NewSendPort](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/3cb398a00c2d08ec87d5805b0645f22c6898ab29/lib/ui/isolate_name_server/isolate_name_server_natives.cc#L26)`/`[Dart_SendPortGetId](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/3cb398a00c2d08ec87d5805b0645f22c6898ab29/lib/ui/isolate_name_server/isolate_name_server_natives.cc#L36C42-L37C1) that IsolateNameServer uses to keep track of opened send ports.
The fix should have been rolled in flutter at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/commit/21da3a80633913db86cfbbb04bd38982b8d15dd5, main channel at this point

> @aam do you know if the fix will also solve #113825 and #119589 ?

I don't think those issues are related to this.

@ringoswy @darshankawar does this reproduce on Android?

If not, guessing it's a SKParagraph issue?

Both Android and iOS are using SkParagraph.

If this is currently only happening on iOS, then it is more likely to be related to Impeller glyph positioning.

@ringoswy @darshankawar does this reproduce with [Impeller off](https://docs.flutter.dev/perf/impeller#ios)?
```
$ flutter run --no-enable-impeller
```

@jonahwilliams Do we need to bisect this still?  It's been months.  I think bisecting will only help us if we want to cherry-pick.  Let me know and I can do it if that's what it takes to close this.  I verified this is still fixed at `main`.

> but not displays marker in mobile browser

Is this on android chrome or iOS safari ?

Also, can you provide us only minimal but complete reproducible without third party implementation which still shows the reported behavior ?

147702.comment_body:
Thanks for the report. I checked this and saw different results below:

- Flutter stable 3.19.6: 
   - The issue is reproduced in `debug` mode. 
   - I checked this a few times in `release` mode and couldn't reproduce the exception but I see callback wasn't being called
- Flutter master `3.22.0-21.0.pre.20`: 
    - The issue is not reproduced in `debug` mode and the callback is called as expected.
    - In `release` mode, the result is the same on Flutter stable 3.19.6

But when I remove `wakelock_plus` usage from main.dart file, I don't see issue anymore on Flutter stable 3.19.6 in debug mode (in release mode, it stays the same as above). Could this package relate? Could you also check this again with these cases to make sure we see the same results?

I also see the same result at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/109910#issuecomment-1223417367 where the exception didn't occur but the camera stream callback wasn't called. Labeling this for further insights.

Related document: https://developer.android.google.cn/reference/kotlin/androidx/activity/result/contract/ActivityResultContracts.PickMultipleVisualMedia

</details>

Just to clarify the truncation problem I'm seeing means that at a certain finger position at the right of the screen the zoomed in view will just disappear (as opposed to drawing a segment of what it should be rendering).  So it seems like a coverage issue, not a rendering issue as Jonah suspects.

Would it be appropriate to allow filters to scale their coverage hint?  So in this problem case, the coverage hint is `(x: 0, y: 0, width: 320, height:320)`. This is the clip region relative to the subpass.  That hint is the same regardless of the size of thing being rendered.  No math effects the coverage hint beyond just intersecting it with the filter coverage.  Should we have something like `coverage_hint = boundingRect(coverage_hint * filter_transform)`?

Here is the problem numbers for consideration:
- coverage_hint: ((0, 0), (320, 320))
- coverage: ((-2613, -2933), (2250, 4002))
- those don't intersect, so nothing is drawn

The matrix filter is scaling by 3 and translating.

Looking at these numbers it seems that transforming the coverage hint could get them to overlap.  However it seems the filter coverage isn't in the appropriate coordinate space, maybe it's missing the offset of the subpass?

I was able to verify that the failing test in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/147807#issuecomment-2115861302 is a proper reproduction of the "truncation problem".  I have it fixed up and I'm cleaning it up for review.
The fix was reverted for a failure in a framework golden test: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52880#issuecomment-2118611765

Thank you for the report. As documented, the transitionDelegate is only considered for Page transitions (i.e. when the [`pages`](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Navigator/pages.html) property changes), not for regular Route transitions:

> The delegate used for deciding how routes transition in or off the screen during the [pages](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Navigator/pages.html) updates.

https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Navigator/transitionDelegate.html

I wasn't able to reproduce this using Xcode 15.0 and newer. I suspect this only happens on Xcode 14.

Could you try the following workarounds:

1. Could you try pinning to `package:image_picker_ios` version 0.8.10 or older? This is before Swift Package Manager support was added
2. Could you try upgrading to Xcode 15.0 or newer?

Please let us know which of these options, if any, fix this issue. That'll help us understand this build error better.

This is working as intended because you're not including an Overlay widget in the tree and the close button of the snackbar (`showCloseIcon: true`) requires an Overlay to be present so it can show itself if necessary.

By default, MaterialApp introduces a Navigator, which includes an Overlay. However, `MaterialApp.builder` is a hook to wrap the Navigator (provided as `child` to `MaterialApp.builder`) with other widgets and therefore the `context` passed to `MaterialApp.builder` does not include an Overlay. If you want to use this `context` for anything that needs an Overlay (like showing a snackbar with a close button that has a tooltip) you'll have to introduce the Overlay yourself (or use a `context` that is a descendant of the `Navigator` introduced by MaterialApp).

This didn't crash in 3.19.5 because the tooltip for the close button of the snack bar was only introduced in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/143934 as an accessibility fix. Not having the tooltip was a bug that made the snackbar less accessible.

This is documented on the `MaterialApp.builder` property (https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/MaterialApp/builder.html): 

> Unless a Navigator is provided, either implicitly from builder being null, or by a builder including its child argument, or by a builder explicitly providing a Navigator of its own, or by the routerDelegate building one, widgets and APIs such as Hero, Navigator.push and Navigator.pop, will not function.
>
> Material specific features such as showDialog and showMenu, and widgets such as Tooltip, PopupMenuButton, also require a Navigator to properly function.

What additional information would have made this clearer?

Spoke more with @camsim99 offline - we define the output path before starting the video but that output path is only referenced internally until we return the file. It should be possible for us to determine the file type after recording has stopped, but before returning from [`stopVideoRecording`](https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/main/packages/camera/camera_android_camerax/lib/src/android_camera_camerax.dart#L996), so that we return a file with the correct extension.

148447.comment_body:
Thanks for the report @flrnsbt 
I am unable to replicate this on macOS. Are you using physical keyboard to input numeric data ?

Thanks for the update @flrnsbt 
Can you also switch to latest master and re-run your scenario to check if it still persists in it as well ?

flutter channel master
flutter upgrade

Thanks for the update. I tried running sample TextField() on web using mac desktop and entering numeric data using physical keyboard, but didn't observed the reported error. Looks like it is specific to Linux + chrome setup. Also, going by @flrnsbt's observation that it didn't occur in previous stable (3.19.6) but occuring in 3.22.0, I'll label this as regression and for team's input / attention.

I can't reproduce the error above but I can see the result at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/148447#issuecomment-2114536019 on my Linux machine in which only 0 and 5 keys are written out (same result to a deduplicated issue at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/148544).

I reproduced this on Flutter Web target platform with a keyboard with numpad keys. This doesn't occur on Flutter Linux app  and Flutter Web target on macOS machine.

The issue doesn't appear on the previous Flutter stable release 3.19.6.

148472.comment_body:

> ```

Can you try by running `./gradlew :app:compileDebugJavaWithJavac` and re-run to see if it helps ?



> 5\. Run `flutter drive --driver=test_driver/integration_test.dart --target=integration_test/video_player_test.dart` integration tests and confirm success

Instead of `flutter drive`, can you try directly `flutter test integration_test` and see if it helps further ?

It may also be related to the gradle version + java version you are using that you can check and confirm.

<details>
<summary>complete sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

/// Flutter code sample for [Switch].

void main() => runApp(const SwitchApp());

class SwitchApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const SwitchApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      theme: ThemeData.light(useMaterial3: true),
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Switch Sample')),
        body: const Center(
          child: SwitchExample(),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class SwitchExample extends StatefulWidget {
  const SwitchExample({super.key});

  @override
  State<SwitchExample> createState() => _SwitchExampleState();
}

class _SwitchExampleState extends State<SwitchExample> {
  bool light = true;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(
      mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
      children: <Widget>[
        Switch(
          value: light,
          onChanged: (bool value) {
            setState(() {
              light = value;
            });
          },
        ),
      ],
    );
  }
}
```

framework version: 0b3a88f218147510bfff696d241bb1fab4322c01

148617.comment_body:
Hi @Rexios80 
I checked this on a new Flutter project without any additional plugins/packages and I didn't see the issue. Steps I did (following docs on https://docs.flutter.dev/platform-integration/web/wasm):

1. Build with wasm: `flutter build web --wasm`
2. activate `dhttpd`: `flutter pub global activate dhttpd`
3. Hosting the web: `dhttpd '--headers=Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy=credentialless;Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy=same-origin'`

I tried the same on your project but it built with a long error. Since your project is using many dependencies, please narrow it down to see if one of them may cause this. Thanks!

I've narrowed it down to `pub_api_client`, but I'm not sure it's that package's fault either. There are no references to `identityHashCode` in that code base. There are other packages with weird issues as well:
- https://github.com/flyerhq/flutter_chat_ui/issues/596#issuecomment-2119160655

That package has perfectly valid code that is failing to compile to WASM. There isn't anything to work off of from either the Flutter debugger or the web dev tools besides that cryptic error without any other information.

>I've narrowed it down to pub_api_client, but I'm not sure it's that package's fault either. 

Does that mean the issue disappeared after you removed it from project? You can also check [Requires JS-interop to access browser and JS APIs](https://docs.flutter.dev/platform-integration/web/wasm#js-interop-wasm) to filter out which packages haven't been migrated to support wasm compilation yet.
If it was a web package migration issue wouldn't the app not even compile? This is a runtime error.

I will mark this as an issue with `flutter run --wasm` to get insights from others. Reproduced the error above `WebAssembly.compileStreaming()` on the latest master channel with given sample code.

This is working as intended and documented on [other parts of the API](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Image/Image.memory.html):

> The scale argument specifies the linear scale factor for drawing this image at its intended size and applies to both the width and the height. For example, if this is 2.0, it means that there are four image pixels for every one logical pixel, and the image's actual width and height (as given by the [dart:ui.Image.width](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/Image/width.html) and [dart:ui.Image.height](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/Image/height.html) properties) are double the height and width that should be used when painting the image (e.g. in the arguments given to [Canvas.drawImage](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/Canvas/drawImage.html)).

We should also include this note in the other Image constructors (and RawImage).

Thanks for the report @sinnoorc 
It seems to be OS version specific, as I wasn't able to replicate this on iOS 15.
Can you check if this only occurs using impeller or without it as well ? You can disable it as below in `info.plist` and re-run your scenario to confirm.

```

<key>FLTEnableImpeller</key>
<false/>

```

I can reproduce this issue on iOS 17.4 emulator (haven't checked this on a physical device as I don't have one). I narrowed this down a bit and see the issue seems to appear with [AnimatedBuilder](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/AnimatedBuilder-class.html) which is used internally inside CircularProgressIndicator.

The issue occurs on the latest stable 3.22.0 and master channel whilst it works normally on the previous 3.19.6.

This seems to only happen on iOS as I don't see the issue on Android (enabled Impeller).

I was able to verify that the flickering doesn't happen on device (iPhone SE 2022 - iOS 17.4.1) at 8eba63f29b8b26931496807cb4fc2fed8e4e3258 I was also able to verify that the flickering doesn't happen on iPhone 15 Pro on iOS 17.4 (Xcode 15) at the same hash. I was able to test on iPhone 15 Pro on iOS 17.5 (Xcode 15.4) at the same hash and it also worked:
I tested with 3.22 stable as well and I don't see the flickering on the iPhone 15 Pro iOS 17.5 simulator.

Okay, here's what we know:
1) The glitch doesn't happen on device
1) The glitch only happens on some macs

Here's the next question:  For the people that are seeing it, what kind of Mac are you using.  I have an M1 Max.

This is sounding like an Apple bug unfortunately.  Someone that can reproduce it should file an apple bug and try downgrading xcode to 15.3 for now.  I'm unable to reproduce the problem so I can't test if that is a valid workaround.  If someone does verify that, could they post that info here please for others?

> I am running on a Mac with an Intel chip.

That's what I was afraid of.  I don't know how successful you will be at getting Apple to address this since it has stopped selling intel macs a while ago.  I'm going to close this since there is nothing we can do from flutter's side to address this.

The workaround is to test on device if you have an intel mac.
Reopening this so the bot doesn't lock this in case more information comes in.

Reproduced this on an Intel Mac Mini.  Bisected it to https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/431d64dbb4b8c3c56e37f5a8cf8f72aa789dc97c

@jonahwilliams

> I don't know if FML_OS_IOS is enabled for simuators as well

`FML_OS_IOS` will be defined as 1 for any target of iOS (iphone, ipad) and that includes builds that run on simulators or devices.

In the scenario where this happens it looks like `HostBuffer` is reusing device buffers before Metal is done consuming them.

Tried adding some logging to `HostBuffer::Reset` and an `addCompletedHandler` callback on the `MTLCommandBuffer`.  The rate of `HostBuffer::Reset` calls exceeds the completion handler calls.

Each `HostBuffer::Reset` advances the `HostBuffer`'s frame index.  The frame index rotates among a pool of `kHostBufferArenaSize` (currently 3) sets of buffers that are reused.  So if `HostBuffer::Reset` is not evenly matched with frame completion then eventually the frame index will wrap around to a buffer that is still in use by Metal.

I was able to get this to run smoothly on the Intel Mac Mini iOS simulator with this patch:
* add an `fml::Semaphore` initialized with count=3 to `surface_mtl.mm`
* call `Wait` on the semaphore before the `CAMetalLayer.nextDrawable` call in `SurfaceMTL::GetMetalDrawableAndValidate`
* in `SurfaceMTL::Present` call `command_buffer.addCompletedHandler` with a callback that signals the semaphore
I strongly advise not turning off Impeller on your production app just for the sake of not seeing glitching in debug mode on a simulator...
The fix is available on Flutter 3.22.3.  Thank you!

148673.comment_body:
Hi @JoanSchi, I couldn't reproduce this issue on both Android emulator and physical device. I use Pixel 4 API 30, emulator. Maybe this is OS-specific issue, what is the Android OS version of your emulator?
Demo issue not reproducible
Could you also check if the issue only occurs if you put TextField(s) inside CustomScrollView? Does it occur without using CustomScrollView?

After disabling floating keyboard, I can reproduce the issue on both Android emulator and physical device. Some other tests:

- This also happens without using CustomScrollView. 
- Only happens in landscape mode
- I also checked this on iOS app but it didn't appear there. 

<details>
<summary>Demo</summary>

<details>
<summary>Sample code without CustomScrollView</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: Text(widget.title),
        ),
        body: const SingleChildScrollView(
          child: Column(
            children: [
              SizedBox(
                height: 72.0,
                child: Row(children: [
                  Expanded(child: TextField(textInputAction: TextInputAction.next)),
                  SizedBox(
                    width: 8.0,
                  ),
                  Expanded(child: TextField()),
                ]),
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ));
  }
}

```
</details>

I also did an additional check on Android native using XML layout below (the first EditText uses `android:imeOptions="actionNext"` in order to focus on next EditText), the issue doesn't appear. The keyboard persists when focusing on the 2nd text field.

<details>
<summary>Android native sample code (XML layout)</summary>

Note: `flagNoExtractUi` attribute is just to prevent the text field from being stretched out horizontally in landscape mode when keyboard is visible.

```xml
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:id="@+id/linearLayout"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    android:layout_gravity="center"
    android:gravity="center"
    android:orientation="horizontal">

    <EditText
        android:id="@+id/et1"
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:layout_weight="1"
        android:hint="Name"
        android:imeOptions="actionNext|flagNoExtractUi"
        android:inputType="text"
        android:maxLines="1"
        android:nextFocusDown="@id/et2" />


    <EditText
        android:id="@+id/et2"
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:layout_weight="1"
        android:hint="Address"
        android:imeOptions="flagNoExtractUi"
        android:maxLines="1" />

</LinearLayout>
```
</details>

Hi @Pratiksha0796 
I checked this using [package example](https://github.com/flutter/packages/tree/main/packages/in_app_purchase/in_app_purchase/example) with the modification as above to print out the verificationData but I can get the decoded data without error. 

What is your current `in_app_purchase` package version? And which platform are you getting the issue? (Android, iOS, macOS).

Please share the entire error trace if any. Thanks!

My IAP setup on iOS has another issue so I can't check this currently. Could you try upgrading the package to the latest version and retry? Hi @FireBird4 
Could you share a complete and minimal sample code for this? I see the dialog background color is `surfaceContainerHigh` already:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/a14f74ff3a1cbd521163c5f03d68113d50af93d3/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/dialog.dart#L1657

I see, I think it should be like this:

```dart
color: backgroundColor ?? dialogTheme.backgroundColor ?? defaults.backgroundColor ?? Theme.of(context).dialogBackgroundColor,
```
<details>
<summary>Sample code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

/// Flutter code sample for [showDialog].

void main() => runApp(const ShowDialogExampleApp());

class ShowDialogExampleApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const ShowDialogExampleApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: const DialogExample(),
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(
          seedColor: Colors.green,
          surface: Colors.black,
          surfaceContainerHigh: Colors.red,
        ),
        // dialogTheme: const DialogTheme(
        // 	backgroundColor: Colors.blue,
        // ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class DialogExample extends StatelessWidget {
  const DialogExample({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('showDialog Sample')),
      body: Center(
        child: OutlinedButton(
          onPressed: () => _dialogBuilder(context),
          child: const Text('Open Dialog'),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }

  Future<void> _dialogBuilder(BuildContext context) {
    return showDialog<void>(
      context: context,
      builder: (BuildContext context) {
        return AlertDialog(
          title: const Text('Basic dialog title'),
          content: const Text(
            'A dialog is a type of modal window that\n'
            'appears in front of app content to\n'
            'provide critical information, or prompt\n'
            'for a decision to be made.',
          ),
          actions: <Widget>[
            TextButton(
              style: TextButton.styleFrom(
                textStyle: Theme.of(context).textTheme.labelLarge,
              ),
              child: const Text('Disable'),
              onPressed: () {
                Navigator.of(context).pop();
              },
            ),
            TextButton(
              style: TextButton.styleFrom(
                textStyle: Theme.of(context).textTheme.labelLarge,
              ),
              child: const Text('Enable'),
              onPressed: () {
                Navigator.of(context).pop();
              },
            ),
          ],
        );
      },
    );
  }
}

```
</details>

148850.comment_body:
> 3\. Run `flutter run --debug -d chrome --wasm`

@Rexios80 According to https://docs.flutter.dev/platform-integration/web/wasm#only-build-support, currently wasm has only build support and not `flutter run`, so could be that you are seeing the reported behavior.
I am running the latest Flutter main commit which does support `flutter run`. Should I not report issues with using it?

Thanks for the feedback. It seems the said command doesn't work in stable yet, so I thought to refer the documentation if that's the case, but I checked in master which ran the command and printed false as below: Keeping the issue open and labeling for team's tracking.
The `--debug` flag does not seem to work for `dart2wasm` either. That is probably the root cause of this.

> The --debug flag does not seem to work for dart2wasm either. That is probably the root cause of this

The Dart2Wasm compiler is only used for `--profile` / `--release` builds (just like Dart2JS). For iterative development DDC (which also compiles to JS) compiler is used.

The fact that `flutter run -d chrome` accepts the `--wasm` flag may be not intentional  /cc @yjbanov @eyebrowsoffire

> We may want to add an error message if someone attempts to use --wasm and --debug together, it is currently not a valid combination.

@eyebrowsoffire Currently `flutter run -d chrome --wasm` runs and it even claims it uses hot-reload:
```
% flutter run -d chrome --wasm
Launching lib/main.dart on Chrome in debug mode...
Compiling lib/main.dart for the Web...                             798ms
✓ Built build/web

🔥  To hot restart changes while running, press "r" or "R".
For a more detailed help message, press "h". To quit, press "q".
```
That cannot be intentional. Either it should warn/error on the `--wasm` flag or it should run in release mode with wasm and not claim it supports hot-reload
Are we saying that there is no way to compile to WASM in such a way that kDebugMode would be true and that is intentional?
Is there an existing issue to support debug mode in WASM builds or should we repurpose this one to be it?
@eyebrowsoffire sorry for the ping but I would like to clarify this if possible. Should we create an issue to support debug mode in WASM builds or is there already an existing issue for that?

@eyebrowsoffire What is the reason we can't just make `kDebugMode` true with `flutter run --wasm` and false with `flutter run --wasm --release`? Are there bigger implications that I'm not seeing? The biggest use-case for this in my projects is using Firebase emulators when `kDebugMode` is true, which makes testing locally with WASM really annoying.
Confirmed `kDebugMode` is true when running `flutter run -d chrome --wasm` on Flutter master

I checked this on the latest Flutter 3.22.1 but I see `isDrawerOpen` returns true as expected but the main page is popped. The same result on Flutter 3.19.6.

149050.comment_body:
Thanks for the report. I observed that the reported behavior is consistent across mobile platforms (Android, iOS) but different in web and desktop platforms.
The reported behavior occurs with IconButton's variants such as `filled`, `outlined` and `filledTonal` only if we wrap these widgets with `Material`. Without wrapping, the icon rendering is consistent across all platforms.

149099.comment_body:
Thanks for the update. Using above details, I was able to verify and replicate the reported behavior. Looks like it is related to unicode text.
Also learned more about full-width space:

`Full-width is used for fields where you will need to type Japanese letters (your mail address, etc.)—in which case, the alphanumeric must be input using full-width. `

Since it worked in latest stable but not in current, I'll label this as regression.

Reproduced this on the web platform.

This started with a Dart SDK change that maps Dart timeline APIs to the web user timing API (`performance.mark`/`performance.measure`)

(see https://dart.googlesource.com/sdk/+/af8fb2cd73c3b5ec96a39c59e85f470efe78d8a4)

The Flutter framework logs several events for each frame in the animation.  Chrome apparently does not have any limit on the size of the buffer that stores user timing events.  So the memory usage of the Chrome renderer process grows quickly as the animation runs, and it will grow unbounded.

@kenzieschmoll @elliette Would it be possible to make logging of Chrome timeline events optional in Flutter?

Thanks for tracking down the culprit! 

Some context here:
- These were added to support performance profiling for web apps
- The Flutter framework uses the `dart:developer` Timeline API to track various events ([example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/38406ff85f241d1a4b096782dfc9f7513fb4d09a/packages/flutter/lib/src/foundation/binding.dart#L144))
- If I remember correctly, these are removed for `release` builds, so this should only be occurring for `profile` and `debug` builds. 

I don't think we can make this optional, but I do see in the MDN docs that there is a `clearMarks` and `clearMeasures` API:  https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Performance. The VM implementation (cc @bkonyi) of this is using a circular buffer to store the timeline events to prevent unbounded memory usage. I think the web implementation should probably do something similar using `clearMarks` and `clearMeasures`.

149236.comment_body:
> ```dart
> child: googleSignInPlugin
>                                                   .renderButton(
>                                                 configuration: html
>                                                     .getGoogleSignInButtonConfiguration(
>                                                         constraints.maxWidth),
>                                               ),
> ```

@jaydeepkaria-mi Is this coming from https://pub.dev/packages/google_sign_in ? I tried with it, but couldn't get the implementation correct ?
Can you elaborate on that usage and provide us complete code sample ?
@jaydeepkaria-mi I tried above code but it is not rendering anything, as below: Can you switch to latest master and re-run your scenario to check if the reported error persists in it or not ?

The repro code is a little bit outdated, now that we have [the `web_only` library](https://pub.dev/documentation/google_sign_in_web/0.12.4/web_only/web_only-library.html) in the `google_sign_in_web` package, but I'll take a look.

I suspect the issue is in the users' code, since the `button_tester` application works just fine. Here's the instructions on how to run it (inside the `example` of `google_sign_in_web`):

* https://github.com/flutter/packages/tree/main/packages/google_sign_in/google_sign_in_web/example#button_testerdart

There's two issues here:

1. the `init` method of the plugin needs to have a `clientId`, either passed to `init` as a parameter, or in a `meta` tag ([instructions](https://pub.dev/packages/google_sign_in_web#web-integration)).
2. The `Error ` that is being printed to the JS Console does not reflect the whole text of the failed assertion, making it harder to come up with a fix. It should say something like:
   ```
   ClientID not set. Either set it on a 
   <meta name="google-signin-client_id" content="CLIENT_ID" /> tag,
   or pass clientId when initializing GoogleSignIn
   ```
   This is the [tracking bug in Chrome](https://g-issues.chromium.org/issues/344629939).

---

A possible fix to the repro case in this test is to do the following:

```diff
- await html.googleSignInPlugin.init();
+ await html.googleSignInPlugin.init(
+   clientId: 'anything-so-this-is-not-null',
+ );
```
Please @jaydeepkaria-mi, let me know if the explanation above makes sense so we can close this issue!

149407.comment_body:
@niklasvongarrel thanks for the easy reproduction test.

@dcharkes I think I narrowed it down to c12d0068c5f0bd7383733151d65d99618985e292 (though I'd appreciate a double-check) which changed the version of Xcode the engine is built against from Xcode 14.3.1/iOS 16.4 to Xcode 15.0/iOS 17.  But I'm not sure why that would impact this since it's opening at runtime based on the target device's SDK etc?

I can reproduce the issue on the latest Flutter stable 3.22.1 and master `3.23.0-13.0.pre.69` with the [given sample project](https://github.com/niklasvongarrel/dynamic-library-open-bug/blob/main/test/widget_test.dart) whilst it doesn't occur on the previous release 3.19.6.

Confirmed that this started with https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/d80fe1cb5854851761f94f5637db5db1ae716164

It looks like recent SDK/Xcode versions include some policy changes affecting the default paths used to search for dynamic libraries (see https://forums.developer.apple.com/forums/thread/737920)

Loading this library does work on current builds when using a fully qualified path:
`DynamicLibrary.open('/System/Library/Frameworks/JavaScriptCore.framework/JavaScriptCore');`

> However, I am somewhat weary of trying to deviate from what Apple is doing by default. WDYT @jmagman @vashworth? Maybe the better question to ask is, how does Apple intend standalone apps to reuse frameworks already installed on the host machine?

I agree.  I don't think ffi should radically deviate from how the native system expects loads.  If native apps require an absolute path, then I don't see why Flutter apps should do anything too differently, assuming Flutter's tooling and build systems allow it to be loaded in the same way.

Here's the diff to help highlight the shadow differences:
![ezgif-7-494e54576b](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/30870216/462ee474-84bc-40af-8424-81d7c55fca77)


In impeller the shadow is missing from the top of the item.  Also on the top left and right corners, left and right of them, the shadow is not consistent with the middle left and right of the shadows.

https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/53130 won't have helped this since in the end it just changed the math for gaussian, not rrect_blur.  I probably should have changed the title.

I reran the test at 3dd40156afb6f878597d7018ea769b752ca5b115 on an iphone se and the differences aren't so stark as the above example.

![ezgif-7-ad6aaa73f8](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/30870216/183336de-53e0-424e-a3db-7050dc4bb183)

The weird artifact where the middle is thicker is still present.  It isn't that noticeable.  The thing that seems to be the most wrong is the lack of any shadow at the top of the widget.
I filed a separate issue for the lack of a gap between the column items (although that didn't show up in the other screenshots above): https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/149689

Here's the frame capture for the render.  Aaaaaactually, jonah was right in that https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/53130 would have effected this potentially (positively).  The top and bottom shadows seem to be rendered with the gaussian blur, the middle one is using the rrect_blur.  So the slight fatness of the middle shadow is a discrepancy between those 2.  This also explains why the difference isn't as apparent after the change.  I would still like us to have more of a shadow on top of the widget.

[column3materials.gputrace.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15571137/column3materials.gputrace.zip)

I looked at this a bit and I'm going to consider it closed as a result of https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/53130.  I think the results look good and the wonkiness seen in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/149474#issuecomment-2148399150 doesn't show up anymore.  I'm also going to file an issue about the lack of any shadow at the top of the widget.  I worry that that the lack of the hairline shadow at the top could cause accessibility issues.

![yes-scale](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/30870216/ceeeb9e7-1eaf-4878-bca2-bf58141f9710)
Issue filed for shadow at the top: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/149757

> I now have a reduced test case in a playground that does reproduce the issue

@chinmaygarde do you have the playground test somewhere?

> > I now have a reduced test case in a playground that does reproduce the issue
> 
> @chinmaygarde do you have the playground test somewhere?

Nevermind, it was simple to replicate.

It seems Skia provides a mechanism for developers to decide if they want pixel aligned glyphs or not, it's called hinting (see https://forum.vvvv.org/t/letters-jumping-in-skia-text-animation/16776).  That's where most of the jiggling is coming from.

`impeller::Font` does not have an equivalent to hinting.  It seems like it should.  There is no equivalent in dart:ui TextStyle either.

It seems like we should surface this to the user so they can decide if they want pixel snapping or not (eg no pixel snapping if they want smooth animations).  Based on this bug report it seems like the skia renderer's default value was no pixel snapping, whereas impeller's is pixel snapping.

I discovered there is an Android API for setting up text to be more easily animated (see [LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/graphics/Paint#LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG), "SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG should be used in conjunction with this flag to prevent glyph positions from snapping to whole pixel values as scale factor is adjusted.").

`LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG` and [`SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/graphics/Paint#SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG) seem to map to `SkFont::isSubpixel()` and `SkFont::isLinearMetrics()`.

I put in a print statement in `DisplayListParagraphPainter::drawTextBlob` before the SkTextBlob is converted to an `impeller::TextFrame` to see what those settings were in the reproduction code.

Here's what was printed out:
```
E/flutter (15171): [ERROR:flutter/third_party/txt/src/skia/paragraph_skia.cc(88)] linear metrics: 0 hinting: 1 isSubpixel: 1
```

To have the most cleanly animated text I would expect `isSubpixel == 1 && isLinearMetrics == 1`.  I wanted to make sure that's not what we were comparing against.  Having "slight" font hinting seems to switch a knob to have less adjustment from pixel snapping.  I'm not familiar with Skia's math here.  My intuition is hinting is like 9 part image drawing, there are built in expansion and compression areas to get things to line up to be rasterized.  There is no equivalent in Impeller that I know of.  I know `isSubpixel` and `isLinearMetrics` are orthogonal.  Previously they were just one setting (isLinearMetrics) to help people animate text, but they split it up.

We are currently reading in the `isSubpixel` value in a SkFont in `MakeTextFrameFromTextBlobSkia`.  It is used for the `ComputeSubpixelPosition` computation.  I've noticed this is a difference between the Playgrounds reproductions and what is actually happening in the original reproduction.  The playground tests don't seem to have a subpixel offset other than (0,0).  This may be related to the font being used in Playground tests.  So this is going to be something to keep in mind when we write a test for this fix.

`isLinearMetrics` and `getHinting` are ignored in `MakeTextFrameFromTextBlobSkia`.

<details>

## patch
```diff
--- a/engine/src/flutter/third_party/txt/src/skia/paragraph_skia.cc
+++ b/engine/src/flutter/third_party/txt/src/skia/paragraph_skia.cc
@@ -84,6 +84,13 @@ class DisplayListParagraphPainter : public skt::ParagraphPainter {
     size_t paint_id = std::get<PaintID>(paint);
     FML_DCHECK(paint_id < dl_paints_.size());
 
+    for (SkTextBlobRunIterator run(blob.get()); !run.done(); run.next()) {
+      FML_LOG(ERROR)                                             //
+          << "linear metrics: " << run.font().isLinearMetrics()  //
+          << " hinting: " << static_cast<int>(run.font().getHinting())
+          << " isSubpixel: " << run.font().isSubpixel();
+    }
+
 #ifdef IMPELLER_SUPPORTS_RENDERING
```

</details>

I've added a print statement to the engine to print out the mvp being passed into the glyph vertex shader.  It prints out:
```
E/flutter ( 6382): [ERROR:flutter/impeller/entity/contents/text_contents.cc(232)] mvp: (
E/flutter ( 6382):        0.001852,       0.000000,       0.000000,      -1.000000,
E/flutter ( 6382):        0.000000,      -0.000880,       0.000000,       1.000000,
E/flutter ( 6382):        0.000000,       0.000000,       0.000000,       0.000006,
E/flutter ( 6382):        0.000000,       0.000000,       0.000000,       1.000000,
E/flutter ( 6382): )
```

This verifies my theory that we are pixel snapping to logical pixels, not real pixels since this orthographic matrix corresponds to a resolution of 540x1136 on my pixel 4a which is 1080x2340.  My PR at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/161445 addresses this by doing the layout math in floating-point logical pixels, then in the vertex shader converting to real pixels, performing the pixel snap, then converting back to normalized coordinates.  However it isn't acceptable yet because the way we render text, we are very susceptible to rendering glyphs in such a way that that we are squishing the texture being drawn, thus missing some horizontal or vertical line of the glyph.

I think what we are potentially missing is adjusting the texture coordinates appropriately when we are performing the pixel snapping.

No, I was wrong.  I forgot the orthographic projection is going to [-1,1] not [0,1].  So with that orthographic projection `M *(0,0,0,1) = (-1, 1, 0 1)` and `M * (1024, 0, 0, 1) = (1, 1, 0 1)`.  We are not rounding in logical pixels.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162049 has landed on `main` now.  I believe there are a couple of compounding issues that cause this bug so I'm not sure if this is 100% satisfactory, please try it out.

Part of verifying that this helped was to scale text by a small amount from the top left corner and make sure that a glyph didn't jump more than the expected 1 pixel.  That would explain some of the visual artifacts where glyphs are jumping more than expected.

Thanks @mrcendre.  I noticed the weird scaling too.  I think I have that captured in a unit test though so maybe we can track that down ( https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/162348 ).

The uv math adjustment is getting reverted unfortunately.  Another thing that complicates this is that we are getting different math on Android vs iOS so while it improved iOS it created a problem on Android.  I think this is because Skia is delegating to different text renderers.

I'm hoping that https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162049 + https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162351 + a fix for https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/162348 will get us where we need to be.  Unfortunately the first one regressed Android, the second one can't land yet since it regresses right-to-left text, and the 3rd one hasn't been written yet.  I haven't given up hope yet though.  The ace up our sleeve is adding a lever to turn on linear metrics at the flutter level, that will be a last resort though.

Here is the test with https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162415 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162351, which should land shortly.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42a2867b-835e-48e8-8342-74170ee2770b

This is looking a lot better in terms of glyph vertical jumping and glyph consistency.  You can see that even more with the playground listed in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/149652#issuecomment-2597197331

What I'm seeing as a potential avenue for inquiry is the horizontal spacing between glyphs.  If you look at the whitespace between the "a" and "d" it oscillates.  Things would look better if it just would favor one way or the other.  This might be a `round` call that should be a `floor`?

That can be seen in the playground too when focusing on the whitespace between the "t" and the "h".

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11dcd600-a218-44dd-84c2-456d3cb419b9

So, here is a case where the scale is increasing, but the space between 2 glyphs is decreasing. We should expect the spacing to be equal or greater when the scale is increasing: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7d16894f-7ea4-40ee-a5fa-d24fa4f6892a) If you do a frame capture you can see at -0.046x there are 16 pixels between the `u` and the `i`, at -0.046x there are 15 pixels. ## -0.046 ## -0.045

Okay, I just landed one more fix that reduces the amount of horizontal spacing jumping between glyphs when scaling text.  

Here's what we've landed so far, in review:

## Worked and landed
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162415 - Fixed stretching of glyphs
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162351 - Removed floating point inaccuracies in positioning glyphs, helping with vertical alignment
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162555 - Increased the resolution of the glyph atlas, helping with glyph spacing oscillation while animating scale

## Didn't land
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/161445 - Supposed to be a golden test with which to measure all efforts, it was showing conflicting results though so we've opted for more specialized, smaller tests.
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162480 - In theory this should give us more accurate results like https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162351 did.  I couldn't come up with a test that proves it though.  I can't visually see a difference either.
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162049 - This landed and was reverted when it hurt Android fidelity.  The thesis behind it is still reasonable, but I'm not sure sure it will make a difference.  One thing it was trying to improve was the aspect ratio for the uvs, that was better accomplished in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162415.

Looking at what scaled fonts looks like after landing those in our tests I'm seeing better consistency of glyphs, better vertical glyph consistency, less jumping in horizontal jumping between glyphs.  There still is some oscillating between glyphs though and when compared with the OP video, the difference is unfortunately subtle.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71076ce0-920f-407b-9122-c5a9c1f8cf94

One thing I haven't look at at all is the subpixel codepath.  In theory that should make this look better.  I didn't investigate it at all since I was seeing problems without it.  Looking at it in playgrounds though the subpixel code doesn't seem to have any positive effect on the results like one would expect.  So that may be a good next thing to investigate.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ddf6f974-45ae-48e9-a0d5-4a0faf4394a6

I didn't want to mention it until it merged since there can always be surprises in golden testing but you beat me to it.  We solved it.

I wasn't sure if we were going to find it, there were so many places where a bug could hide in the font rendering.  Last year I had to rewrite the blur code because that was easier than trying to find the bugs, but it was all contained to 1 file.  The font rendering is spread out across multiple places and has multiple points of entry.

Anyway, that was a trip.  Thanks for your patience and support.  See you on the next bug, hopefully it won't require the same investment to fix.

Thanks for filing the issue. I can see the issue on Web HTML renderer as well. On other platforms (tested on Android and macOS apps, it outputs the same as Web canvaskit renderer - expected result) 1. Looking at the `ActionChip` source code, I see the label has the same color (`onSurface`) even with enabled or disabled state: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/63daac2a51deb76ab13814260fc8ae9f8efc7451/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/action_chip.dart#L269-L273 2. I also check other chip widgets (ActionChip, Chip, RawChip, InputChip, ChoiceChip, FilterChip), and here are results: | html | canvaskit | | --------------- | --------------- | |
Sample code (with other chip widgets) ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp( const MaterialApp( home: Material( child: Column( mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [ ActionChip( onPressed: null, label: Text('ActionChip - onPressed: null'), ), Chip( avatar: Icon(Icons.favorite_rounded), label: Text('Chip - onDeleted: null'), onDeleted: null, ), RawChip( avatar: Icon(Icons.favorite_rounded), label: Text('RawChip - isEnabled: false'), onSelected: null, isEnabled: false, onDeleted: null, ), InputChip( avatar: Icon(Icons.favorite_rounded), label: Text('InputChip - isEnabled: false'), isEnabled: false, onPressed: null, onDeleted: null, ), ChoiceChip( avatar: Icon(Icons.favorite_rounded), label: Text('ChoiceChip - onSelected: null'), selected: false, onSelected: null, ), FilterChip( label: Text('FilterChip - onSelected: null'), onSelected: null, selected: false, ), ], ), ), ), ); } ```
- The issue also occurs on `Chip` and `FilterChip` widgets. - `Chip`: it uses `RawChip` under the hood. But `RawChip` has an additional `isEnabled` parameter that consolidates with `onDeleted` to decide whether the chip is enabled or disabled -> This could be working as desired or this should be another issue (proposal) in case we want to disable the text too. - `FilterChip`: The enabled/disabled state is decided by `onSelected` parameter, see [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/63daac2a51deb76ab13814260fc8ae9f8efc7451/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/filter_chip.dart#L226). The sample code has `onSelected: null` already but the text is not grey.

149738.comment_body:
I checked https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/129878 again on the latest version `go_router: ^14.1.4` with sample code at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/129878#issue-1784132072, I see that issue has been fixed. I also print `navigationShell.currentIndex` when switching tabs and see it printed out the correct index.

With the given sample code on this issue, I see `pathParameters` persists with `{id: 0}` when navigating between tabs but `navigationShell.currentIndex` is not changed (always is 0). I think this is a `go_router_builder` rather than `go_router` issue. Labeling this for further insights. The current reproducible version is `go_router_builder: ^2.7.0`.

149791.comment_body:
Hi @LukasKompatscher 
What is your current `video_player` package version? Does this issue only occur on Android or also occur on iOS app?

I don't see you set `httpHeaders` param on the snippet code above. Can you please provide a [completed and minimal reproducible code sample](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example) that doesn’t include 3rd party plugins or complex production code?

Is only `set-cookie` affected or are all headers ignored? Can you share a way to verify this on Android? This looks similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/109379, you can visit and confirm if it is.

Ok, I can reproduce this issue on Android with a local host.

Steps I did to reproduce this:
1. Run a local host with a script [index_videoplayer_header.js](https://github.com/huycozy/local-server-self-signed-nodejs/blob/master/index_videoplayer_header.js) which hosts a video file and has response like this:

```js
response.writeHead(200, { 
  'Content-Type': contentType, 
  'Accept-Ranges': 'bytes',
  'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(content),
  'set-cookie': ['type=ninja']
});
```

```
node index_videoplayer_header.js
```

2. Verify headers are set by going to URL on the browser and check response headers:

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: video/mp4
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Content-Length: 2429896
set-cookie: type=ninja
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2024 05:03:35 GMT
Connection: keep-alive
Keep-Alive: timeout=5
```

3. Run package example [basic.dart](https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/main/packages/video_player/video_player/example/lib/basic.dart) with a modification at initState to print out the headers from URL:

```dart
_controller = VideoPlayerController.networkUrl(
  Uri.parse('http://192.168.1.5:8125/files/Butterfly-209.mp4'),
)..initialize().then((_) {
    // Ensure the first frame is shown after the video is initialized, even before the play button has been pressed.
    setState(() {
      print('httpHeaders: ${_controller.httpHeaders}');
    });
  });
```

It returns an empty array of headers.

149833.comment_body:
Hi @AriasBros 
This should be fixed on the plugin side. The current version of `firebase_app_check` uses `<angle>` include instead of quotes, see https://github.com/firebase/flutterfire/blob/master/packages/firebase_app_check/firebase_app_check/ios/Classes/FLTFirebaseAppCheckPlugin.h#L5-L13.

For reference on a similar fix for 1st party package `image_picker`: https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/5635/files.

Meanwhile, you can try a workaround from this [answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/63648164/5921933).

Please report the issue on [flutterfire](https://github.com/firebase/flutterfire) repository for better support there. Closing the issue. If you disagree, please write in the comments and I will reopen it. Thanks!

>So it seems like there is something different between the iOS project of a Flutter module and the iOS project of a Flutter app.

The CL's are listed in this PR if you want to poke around before monday: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/53182

- It's weird that the png still has the form.  This suggests that the alpha channels and stride are correct.
- I audited the skia CL too, and it doesn't really do much.  There isn't much room for there to be a bug.  It almost seems like there is some switch statement's default branch we are hitting, the bug is not in the changed code but code that wasn't changed.

Hello, thank you for creating this issue! This is a performance optimization that's working as intended. This is platform-specific, which is why you'll see different behaviors on different platforms. Desktop platforms also have this "zero size" first frame.

That first frame with an empty size allows Flutter and your app to initialize themselves before we know the size of the view. This makes the second frame (which is the first frame driven by a vsync) as fast as possible.

Please let us know if you have any other questions or concerns!

@ksokolovskyi Hey there, here are some interesting docs you might find useful:

1. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b98d48549c8752c649ace52ef200e7d51d6d20b1/packages/flutter/lib/src/scheduler/binding.dart#L994-L1016
2. https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/PlatformDispatcher/scheduleWarmUpFrame.html
3. https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/FlutterView/physicalSize.html

   > At startup, the size of the view may not be known before Dart code runs. If this value is observed early in the application lifecycle, it may report [Size.zero](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/Size/zero-constant.html).

I've also sent a pull request to further clarify this situation: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/150464

Please let us know if you have more questions!

    • All required HTTP hosts are available

! Doctor found issues in 1 category.



```

</details>

@stefanschaller due to changes needed for multi-view support, we *did* change the timing of this slightly, and the callback fires when the factory function is run, but slightly before it is actually injected in the DOM.

We documented this here:

* https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/HtmlElementView-class.html#html-lifecycle

There's APIs in the DOM that can be used to detect when the root element of your platform view is injected into the DOM.

----

(Similar to: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/143922)

> I'm not able to fix the example with the provided documentation. Could take a look? @ditman

@stefanschaller are there any errors in the JS console of the browser? Is this browser specific?

> Looks good, I'm sadly not able to show the full screen, since there are some customer related data, but it's working as expected. Thank you!
> 
> I also migrated from an `BodyElement` to an `DivElement` and also removed the `dart:html` import.
> 
> @ditman You could close the issue. Thank you for the great support

Closing this one! Thanks for reporting @stefanschaller, keep them coming!

150078.comment_body:
I think this is caused by your fixed `crossAxisCount` is always set to 2. You should manage to handle it as a dynamic value for your desire, something like this:

```dart
gridDelegate: SliverGridDelegateWithFixedCrossAxisCount(crossAxisCount: box1 ? 2 : 1),
```

Let me know if it works for your case.

It is documented that the number of tiles in cross axis is fixed.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7b6d667701e38c1d9d700e2bcc29bc7a06598c59/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/sliver_grid.dart#L336-L342

I'm more inclined to GridView implementation rather than Visibility issue. If you replace GridView with Row or Wrap widget, the issue will not occur.

>it would be unfeasible and it would be too big to place several ternaries of all the "boxes" within crossAxisCount

How will it be unfeasible? Can you share an example? Does https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/149659 sound like a similar one?

As I explained above, even when you set Visibility's replacement to a `SizedBox.shrink()`, it still takes a space there since there is a fixed `crossAxisCount` by GridView. 

I'm unsure if there is a better solution for this here, I will keep this open for other's input.

Given that this works with previous iOS versions but not 17.5.1, it seems like this might be more a bug in iOS that the webview_flutter plugin.

Please keep us updated with your findings. 

Thank you

Hi @jdvalenzuela1, could you provide a complete minimal reproducible code sample with the js portion as well in a repository that we can clone so that we can properly investigate this issue? Ideally, this would not be your project source, just enough code that we can reproduce the issue and properly investigate it. 

Thank you

There might be something we can change in the compiler - but I would recommend changing representation of language data instead. The current way things are represented is not very reasonable - having multimegabyte map literal in the source file --- among other things it will produce ungodly amount of native code.

Simplest change you could make is to convert it to string and JSON parse it, even better would be to use some more optimized representation.

The problem reported by @TrueHorse is now fixed upstream by dart-lang/sdk@eb88654

The problem reported by @jpohhhh is different, I have made a separate patch for that: https://dart-review.googlesource.com/c/sdk/+/373661
Both reported issues should now be fixed upstream.

150328.comment_body: @BenjiFarquhar > `Notification` from dart:html is not working. Is this code meant to show a notification? This does work. You need to allow notifications to show, as below: Then upon running the given code, it shows the notifications and gets printed in the console as well: ``` A Dart VM Service on Chrome is available at: http://127.0.0.1:52353/_CeaKEIrgOQ= Hello, World! ```

> Can you please show a screenshot of the full page with the notification showing "Hello World!"? Are you running locally in debug mode with `flutter run -d chrome`, as I am?

Interestingly, when I for the first time ran the code, I did see the notification on the top right corner of the screen but I didn't take screenshot or video record it and now when I tried again, I don't see it occuring, so I missed the chance to record it : (

@BenjiFarquhar, I think I found the issue. I think the problem is that on MacOS, **system-level notifications for Chrome are turned off by default!** | | |---| |
Turn these on!
(https://stackoverflow.com/a/75804760) | After turning on notifications on those two, they immediately started showing on the demo app that I proposed above. I have deployed it here as well, it'll be there for a while: * https://dit-tests.web.app (They even stay on the sidebar until dismissed) --- PS: Chrome uses the native MacOS notifications since v59. Here's the announcement of Chrome using the _native notification system on macOS_: * https://developer.chrome.com/blog/native-mac-os-notifications

150339.comment_body:
Thanks for the report. This doesn't seem to be specific to windows, as, on macOS too, I was able to replicate the reported behavior. The entire text gets selected on web when tabbing back and forth, but in desktop platform, the text isn't selected and tabbing back and forth changes the cursor which is located at the end of the text.

I found this old issue which was fixed but most probably was for web : https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/78895
Another maybe related open issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59519

> Whoops sorry @TahaTesser -- I forgot to come back to this issue! Yeah, I wasn't sure whether it was an actual issue considering this is just the standard behavior of overlays. I did notice that the code for closing the menu contains a check for schedulerphase, so I wasn't sure if it was overlooked for opening or if this is expected behavior.
> 
> https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/a3f6a2bdc7e637c9df8f14ceb31aff4edb517b89/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/menu_anchor.dart#L590
> 
> I also wasn't sure if there should be a "declarative" way of opening the menu. For example, web dialogs have an "open" parameter that will trigger their showing, rather than users having to call a function or otherwise having to wait for a frame. But if it's expected behavior, we can close this issue! Sorry for the trouble.

Yes, it was overlooked. Good news is that this issue has been fixed in PR #157612.
I think we should be able to close this issue, but we can wait for confirmation from @davidhicks980 first.

In order to properly address the issue, please provide us runnable but minimal reproducible code sample without third party package implementation, that still triggers the reported log. Also provide the platform (web, desktop, mobile) in which you are seeing this log.

Hi @dullgrass 
It looks like you are on an [add-to-app](https://docs.flutter.dev/add-to-app) scenario, right? 

For the issue to be workable, it needs to be reproducible with a [completed and minimal reproducible code sample](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example) that doesn’t include 3rd party plugins or complex production code. Please provide such a sample and the output of `flutter doctor -v` as well. Thanks!

Looks like the sample code is incomplete. When I click on `Push to flutter` button, new screen is empty:

<details>
<summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/104349824/d48e7fe7-3064-4d3c-8d4b-6105d2ad093a
</details>

Also, I guess you embedded Flutter module using framework method, right? Since it's not CocoaPods method with flutter module project, I can't check if it works on the latest Flutter version or not (your current Flutter version is quite old), so please try upgrading your Flutter SDK to the latest stable 3.22.2 to see if the result has changed.

I also tried with my sample project using CocoaPods and frameworks methods but couldn't reproduce the crash. 

https://github.com/huycozy/Flutter-iOS-native-Add-to-app-optionA/tree/undo-plugin-150408
https://github.com/huycozy/Flutter-iOS-native-Add-to-app-optionB/tree/undo-plugin-150408

@dullgrass @dongfeng52 Can you try with my sample code to see if the issue occurs on your side? 

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/104349824/5adc7808-9dbe-48be-92c6-91331af7d0a0

This changed in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52234 but it looks like it's still happening on the master channel.  We should add an integration test for the full lifecycle.

> the question is whether the FlutterUndoManagerDelegate within FlutterUndoManagerPlugin can be marked with weak.

Actually the delegate was already weak (the code you posted was MRC code and you can see it wasn't retained).  I migrated it to ARC in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52234 and it's now explicitly weak, but I can still reproduce this.

The problem is that at the point of dealloc `_undoManagerDelegate.undoManager` is nil, which means it doesn't actually call `-removeAllActionsWithTarget`.
https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/4bb4c1c2b5a60bb42ecdfde57ff98f001cea1f64/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterUndoManagerPlugin.mm#L31
Have a fix out at https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/53553.  Thank you so much @huycozy and @dullgrass for figuring out the repro steps!

150549.comment_body:
cc @camsim99 

#149294 is related but targeting another implementation package.

Migrating to the camerax implementation is unacceptable to downstream packages since:
1. The `camera_android` is not deprecated.
2. `flutter pub upgrade` will automatically upgrade the package to this broken version and shipped to the user end.
3. Both packages are still experimental (no major version releases yet).

Reproducible using the plugin example app. This indeed works as expected with camera_android: 0.10.9+3 but does not work with 0.10.9+5 | 0.10.9+5 | 0.10.9+3 | | -- | -- | | | |

Then we should continue tracking this with a further reland if I get this correct.

> From triage: We suspect this is a duplicate of #149177

Again, the issue is about the Camera2 implementation not the CameraX.

150774.comment_body:
Hi @mohsinraza-fdev, I think this might be an issue with your debouncing logic. You could at certain points have the text show what you have typed but that would not be the request that is made. You can see what I mean by running your app in the web browser and checking the network requests. 

How did you come up with the logic for the debouncing? I think using a fixed delay might yield a more consistent result.

150831.comment_body:
I see the issue here. The main difference between your and my settings is `synthetic-package` flag. Per [Internationalizing Flutter apps](https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility-and-internationalization/internationalization) docs:

>synthetic-package       Determines whether the generated output files are generated as a synthetic package or at a specified directory in the Flutter project. This flag is true by default. When synthetic-package is set to false, it generates the localizations files in the directory specified by arb-dir by default. If output-dir is specified, files are generated there.

In your case, the flag `synthetic-package` is not declared, meaning it defaults to `true` but the tool doesn't work. When setting it as `synthetic-package: false`, the tool can generate files. Keeping this as a tool issue. We should also update docs on [website](https://github.com/flutter/website) respectively.

Also @reidbaker for general Android guidance. If you're doing add-to-app, we're assuming you have an existing Android app or have general Java/Kotlin familiarity. 

The owner of the engine group, and by extension, engine is the companion object for your MainActivity in your provided example (https://github.com/BunnarithHeang/Flutter-Android-Engine-Issue/blob/cff7b4922c8dc0fcc707d73014215aa2b96452cf/Android/app/src/main/java/com/example/android/MainActivity.kt#L11). 

Companion objects are held by the class, rather than objects of the class. So the companion object and everything it holds are alive for the duration of the program. Seems all working as intended given the program.

Reduced to a simple test case. Will be fixed when https://dart-review.googlesource.com/c/sdk/+/374040 lands.

tldr: fine grained invalidation leads to situations where we loose some CHA related bits on `Class` objects - which leads to bugs down the line (in the original test it was due to type-testing stubs being miscompiled).
This has been fixed upstream by dart-lang/sdk@4d57de8
